name: Debt Advisors
description_short: Phoenix-based debt relief enrollment service offering settlement, consolidation, and credit counseling programs tailored to individual financial situations.
description_long:
Debt Advisors is a debt relief enrollment service that originated in Phoenix, Arizona, and positions itself as a guidance-focused alternative to one-size-fits-all debt solutions. The company built its practice on the recognition that debt relief needs vary significantly by individual circumstance, and that consumers often hesitate to seek help due to the stigma surrounding financial difficulties.

The company offers multiple debt relief pathways including debt settlement, debt consolidation, credit counseling, debt validation/resolution, and bankruptcy guidance (Chapter 7 and 13). They also provide credit repair services, specifically disputing errors on credit reports across all three major bureaus with the stated goal of removing negative marks and increasing credit scores. Their educational content covers credit scoring mechanics, credit report error identification, state usury laws, and debt validation strategies.

Debt Advisors differentiates itself through personalized consultation rather than automated enrollment, claiming "years of experience" ensures clients receive tailored guidance throughout their debt relief journey. The company emphasizes client trust and doing "the right thing" as core values. They hold an A+ BBB rating and operate during limited hours (Monday-Thursday, 9:30 AM-6:00 PM MST), suggesting a smaller, boutique operation.

The company's website lacks specific details about success rates, average debt reduction amounts, fees, program timelines, or detailed case studies. The "About Us" page is incomplete, cutting off mid-sentence. While they reference creditor letters forfeiting collection rights, no actual documentation is visible. The placeholder statistics on their homepage (0 employees, 0 locations, 0% satisfaction) suggest outdated or incomplete website maintenance, raising questions about operational transparency and current business status.
pros: A+ BBB rating demonstrates recognized credibility with Better Business Bureau
Offers multiple debt relief options (settlement, consolidation, counseling, bankruptcy) rather than pushing single solution
Provides debt validation/resolution services to challenge invalid debts
Educational content on credit scores, credit report errors, and state usury laws
Personalized consultation approach acknowledging individual financial situations
Credit repair services across all three credit bureaus
Free debt relief quote and consultation available
cons: Limited business hours (Mon-Thurs 9:30 AM-6:00 PM MST only) restrict accessibility
Homepage contains placeholder statistics (0 employees, 0 locations, 0% satisfaction) indicating poor website maintenance
About Us page is incomplete and cuts off mid-sentence, suggesting unprofessional presentation
No transparent pricing, fee structure, or program cost information disclosed on website
No success rates, case studies, or documented results provided beyond vague reference to creditor letters
No information about company size, years in business, or number of clients served
best_for: Consumers with mixed debt types seeking personalized guidance on which relief strategy fits their situation
Individuals with credit report errors who want to dispute negative marks across all three bureaus
People hesitant about debt help who prefer one-on-one consultation over automated enrollment
Debtors considering multiple options (settlement vs. consolidation vs. bankruptcy) and wanting professional comparison
diagnosis: Debt Advisors is best for consumers with multiple debt types who want personalized guidance to determine which relief strategy (settlement, consolidation, counseling, or bankruptcy) suits their situation, rather than a company that pushes a single solution. However, the incomplete website information, lack of transparent pricing, placeholder statistics, and limited hours suggest either a small operation or poor digital infrastructure—making it difficult to verify current business status or operational capacity before committing.
services: Credit report error dispute and removal
Negative mark removal across three credit bureaus
Educational content on credit scoring and debt options
